title: CLI compensation: Add fab approve command with --all and --interactive modes
description: Add fab approve CLI command to compensate for Dashboard's zero-write positioning. Implements two modes: --all (batch approve all drift entries) and --interactive (per-entry confirmation with diff display). Reuses approveHumanLock() from packages/server and readHumanLock() from packages/server. Register in allCommands index.

files.change: Define citty command with --all and --interactive flags. Load drift entries via readHumanLock(). In --all mode: loop all drift entries, call approveHumanLock() with new_hash=current_hash. In --interactive mode: display file:line, hash diff, prompt y/n per entry, approve on 'y'. Report summary count at end.
Add approve: () => import('./approve.js').then(m => m.default) entry to allCommands

implementation: Create packages/cli/src/commands/approve.ts using defineCommand from citty
Define args: --all (boolean flag) and --interactive (boolean flag) and --target (string, default cwd)
Import readHumanLock from server package (check existing import path pattern in cli commands)
Implement --all mode: filter drift entries (entry.drift === true), call approveHumanLock() for each with new_hash = entry.current_hash, print summary
Implement --interactive mode: for each drift entry, print file:line, expected vs actual hash, prompt y/n via stdin readline, approve on y/yes
Handle case: zero drift entries → print 'No drift entries found' and exit 0

reference.pattern: citty defineCommand pattern with readline prompt
reference.examples: Follow human-lint.ts command structure: defineCommand, args with target, async run. Use same normalizeTarget pattern.

criteria: fab approve --all approves all drift entries (entries where drift=true) in a single invocation
fab approve --interactive prompts for each drift entry and only approves confirmed entries
fab approve with no flags prints usage help (citty built-in)
Zero drift entries case exits cleanly with informational message
Command registered in allCommands and accessible as fab approve
Each approved entry produces a ledger event (via approveHumanLock internal behavior)

rationale.chosen_approach: Reuse existing approveHumanLock() server service directly — logic already correct and tested
decision_factors: human-lint.ts already imports from shared package — CLI→server imports are established pattern
rationale.tradeoffs: CLI must import server package directly — acceptable since CLI package.json likely already depends on server or shared packages

manual_checks: Run fab approve --all on a project with 2 drift entries and verify both are approved
Run fab approve --interactive and verify only 'y' responses trigger approval
Run fab approve --all on clean project (no drift) and verify clean exit message
success_metrics: All drift entries approved in --all mode with correct ledger entries created
Interactive mode processes each entry individually without skipping

risks.description: CLI package may not have direct dependency on server package — import path may fail
risks.mitigation: Check packages/cli/package.json for server dependency; add if missing or use @fenglimg/fabric-shared re-exports
risks.fallback: Move approval logic to shared package and import from there

functions.purpose: Batch approve all drift entries using approveHumanLock()
functions.returns: void, prints count of approved entries

functions.purpose: Prompt per-entry approval with diff display
